message:
feat(connector): Add Aider chat history connector

Implement a new connector for Aider, a popular AI pair-programming
tool that stores conversations in `.aider.chat.history.md` files
within project directories.

The Aider connector parses markdown-based chat history using a
line-based parser that recognizes:
- User messages: Lines starting with "> " prefix
- Assistant responses: Non-prefixed lines following user blocks
- Multi-line user input: Consecutive ">" lines combined into single message
- Aider commands: /add, /drop, /run preserved in content

Key implementation details:
- Uses glob pattern `**/.aider.chat.history.md` for recursive scanning
- Timestamps derived from file mtime (Aider doesn't store per-message ts)
- Workspace set to parent directory of chat history file
- External ID from filename, title includes full path
- Author field mirrors role (user/assistant/system)
- Supports incremental indexing via since_ts file modification check

Connector registration:
- Added to src/connectors/mod.rs module exports
- Registered in indexer pipeline alongside other connectors

Test coverage includes 40+ tests covering:
- Basic parsing and field extraction
- Message sequencing and role alternation
- Empty/whitespace file handling
- Subdirectory scanning
- Special characters and Unicode
- Code blocks and markdown in responses
- Timestamp filtering
- Error handling for missing directories

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
